amd.description: Prof. Storm has a lead on the Beacon - an ancient signal buried in the ruins the old giants left behind. Hail her for each heading, follow the clues, and stay a jump ahead of whatever is hunting the same trail.

lifeforms.lifeform: Expedition lead and xeno-archaeologist. A host-less advisor - hail her from anywhere on the Ultra-Beam (no target selected) for the next lead on the Beacon.

A back-channel ship-dealer working the old marches out of a ramshackle hull. Sells gear nobody else will - some of it even works. A host-less advisor: hail him on the Ultra-Beam (no target selected) to browse his deals. Cash up front, no refunds.
Your chief engineer - keeps this under-gunned little ship flying on scavenged parts and spite. Hail her on the Ultra-Beam for a read on the ship and a piece of her mind.

A Skaraan treasure-broker, hell-bent on selling the Beacon to the highest bidder - and not much fussed whether it's whole or prised off your wreck. She hired the hunters on your tail. Hail her on the Ultra-Beam to deal, or to tell her where to go.

Two hunters in one battered ship on Madame Skarr's contract. One does the talking; the other, Dobbs, does the shooting and can't be bothered with the rest. The voice on your comms when the Second Wind runs you down.

node.speaker: prof_storm
node.line: Storm here, Captain. What do you need?
Go ahead - I'm knee-deep in old star charts.
Talk to me. The trail's still warm.

node.speaker: prof_storm
node.line: Watch yourself out there. The Beacon isn't the only thing hunting these ruins.
Keep the ship quiet and the sensors hot. We find it by being clever, not loud.

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: Eddy's the name, salvage is the game! Step right up, captain.
You look like a soul in need of an upgrade. Lucky you - I'm having a sale.
No refunds, no returns, no questions asked. What'll it be?

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: Every piece genuine salvage, pried off wrecks with my own two hands. Cash up front!
node.choice: That big giant-tech frame - the Resonance Cradle - 500cr

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: For my best customer? I keep the honest stuff under the counter. No gambles, just gear.
You've been good to me, captain. So I'll be good to you. Discount, and no funny business.

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: Pleasure doing business! Bolt it on and don't come crying to me.
Sold! To the captain with more credits than sense.

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: The big ugly frame? Ha! Knew it'd find a buyer eventually. Never could unload the thing - too heavy, too weird, hums when you look at it wrong. All yours, captain. No refunds!
Careful hauling that home. Feels like it's WAITING for something. Gives me the creeps.

node.speaker: crazy_eddy
node.line: Your loss. I'll be here, marinating in the dark.
Fly safe. Or don't - salvage is salvage.

node.speaker: chief_vex
node.line: Vex here. She's holding together. Mostly. What do you need?
Engineering. Talk fast - I've got a coolant line arguing with me.
If you're about to ask me to push the coils past redline again - no. Ask anyway.
node.choice: What do you make of XORN?

node.speaker: chief_vex
node.line: She's a Warpster, not a warship. We win by being somewhere else when the shooting starts.
Keep buying Eddy's junk and I'll keep bolting it on - just don't blame me when it bites.
Give me a real salvage yard and a week and I'd make her sing. Failing that, we improvise.

node.speaker: chief_vex
node.line: XORN outguns us at every turn. Our edge is the jump drive and knowing when to burn it.
If that thing gets a firing solution we don't trade shots, we run. That's not cowardice, that's math.

node.speaker: chief_vex
node.line: I'll be in the engine room. Where else would I be.

node.speaker: madame_skarr
node.line: Ah - the little grave-robber. You've been busy. So have I.
That Beacon has buyers lined up around the sector, captain. Be smart: sell it to me.
My hunters bill by the hour. Save us both the trouble and hand it over.
node.choice: What do you even want, Skarr?

node.speaker: madame_skarr
node.line: The Beacon, of course. Whatever it turns out to be, it's worth more than your ship and your life put together. Storm thinks it's history. I know it's a payday. One of us is a romantic.

node.speaker: madame_skarr
node.line: Mm. Credits now, hunters called off. A pleasure - for the moment.
Don't mistake this for mercy, captain. It's a receipt.

node.speaker: madame_skarr
node.line: Run along. My hunters will keep you company.
We'll speak again. Over the Beacon, or over your wreck.

node.speaker: bounty_crew
node.line: Second Wind here. Madame Skarr's paying good coin for your Beacon. Easy way, or the other way.
Dobbs, say hello to the nice captain. ...Dobbs. Dobbs (bored): Hi. Whatever.
Hand it over and nobody has to explain anything to Skarr. Dobbs: Are we shooting or not. Whatever.

node.speaker: bounty_crew
node.line: Suit yourself. Dobbs, warm up the guns. ...Dobbs. Dobbs: Yeah. Whatever.

quest.description: Prof. Storm's search for the Beacon - which turns out to be no single relic but five pieces the giants scattered across their ruins. Hail her for each lead, gather the pieces, and carry them to the origin to be made whole.
quest.quest: Hail Prof. Storm on the Ultra-Beam (no target selected) for your first lead on the Beacon.
Storm's first heading: an ancient Torgoth channel out at (2, -1), coreward past the old marches. Engage the jump drive from your Quests tab and follow it in.

quest.description: The first of the Beacon's five pieces: its transmitter core, buried in the dead heart of a Torgoth megastation from the age when the Torgoth were giants - docking throats wide enough to swallow your ship whole.

section.title: Objective
section.item: Bring the science array to bear on the derelict
Scan the core to take the Voice
Hail Prof. Storm for the next lead

quest.section: Scavengers found the trail first. A light bounty-hunter wing loiters in the wreck's shadow - clear them or slip past, but do not linger; worse things than scavengers follow this trail.

quest.quest: Storm cross-referenced the Voice against the old charts: the next piece lies in the Kralien marches at (4, 1). Engage and jump.

quest.description: A cold Kralien megastructure, vast and dark, holding the Beacon's LENS - the focusing array that bends the Voice into a single coherent signal. The Kralien built to the same giant scale as the Torgoth; the docking rings alone dwarf a modern fleet.

section.title: Objective
section.item: Scan the Lens from the derelict's core
Hail Prof. Storm once you have it

quest.section: A Torgoth reclamation party got here first and is stripping the outer decks - they want the Lens too. Take it before they do, or take it from them.

quest.quest: Two pieces in hand and Storm sees the shape of it now: the Beacon is an ARRAY, not a relic, and it needs a CRADLE to seat the pieces - one the giants' heirs looted and lost. Of all people, Crazy Eddy has it. Meanwhile the third piece, the Cipher, waits in a nebula-wrapped relay at (6, -2). Engage and jump - and think about paying Eddy a visit.

quest.description: A lone relay drowned in bruise-violet nebula, older than the ruins that pointed to it, holding the Beacon's CIPHER - the key that aligns Voice and Lens into one signal instead of noise. Without it, the other pieces are just scrap that hums.

section.title: Objective
section.item: Slip through the nebula to the relay
Scan the Cipher, then hail Storm

quest.section: The cloud hides more than the relay. Keep the sensors hot and the throttle ready - you are not the only one who followed this signal into the murk.

quest.quest: The pieces are useless loose - they need the CRADLE to seat and resonate them, and Crazy Eddy fenced the only one left. Buy the Resonance Cradle from Eddy - hail him on the Ultra-Beam, or better, drop by his Emporium and buy it in person. Do it any time before the finale; the Beacon can't be lit without it.
Eddy runs his whole racket out of the Spaceship Emporium, a ramshackle bazaar at (3, 3). Engage and jump there to see his stock in person - hail the station to browse the deals.

The Cipher named the last ruin-piece and where it went: the HEART, the Beacon's power source - and XORN hauled it out to a dead salvage yard at (-5, -4). You'll have to pry it loose from under their guns. Engage, and be ready to run.

quest.description: Not a ruin - a kill-yard. XORN dragged the Beacon's HEART, its power source, out to this gutted salvage field and left a heavy screen on it: wrecks, mines, and a wing that answers to no flag but its own.

section.title: Objective
section.item: Reach the Heart through the yard
Scan it, then break for open space and jump

quest.section: XORN is close here and comes fast. This is the last place to be caught lingering - scan the Heart and RUN. Do not try to hold the field.

quest.quest: Every ruin-piece in hand - Voice, Lens, Cipher, Heart - and (you did buy the Cradle?) the frame to seat them. Storm has the origin at last: (0, -7), the deep dark past every chart, where the giants first lit the Beacon. Carry the pieces home. Engage.

quest.description: The place the Beacon was born: a vast, silent array adrift in the deepest dark past every chart, waiting a thousand years for its pieces to come home. Scanning it wakes the array - but the Beacon only sings once every piece is seated.

section.title: Objective
section.item: Scan the origin array to bring it online
Hail Prof. Storm to assemble and light the Beacon

quest.section: XORN will arrive fast to see the Beacon stays silent forever. Hold long enough for Storm to finish - or you will have carried every piece this far for nothing.

goal.citation: At the origin, with XORN's guns lighting the dark, Prof. Storm seated the last piece in the Cradle and woke the Beacon. It did what the giants built it to do a thousand years ago - it called. And in its first clear note was their final warning: the thing that unmade the giants and drove them small and hidden, the thing XORN still answers to. The Beacon is whole, its message is logged, and one small ship flew home with a story the giants died to leave. XORN broke off into the dark. It will be back. So will you.

goals.goal: Gather all five pieces of the Beacon - the four ruin-pieces AND Eddy's Cradle - carry them to the origin, and have Prof. Storm assemble and light it while XORN does everything to stop you.

regions.region: The frontier past the last charted buoy - the old marches, where the giants of an earlier age left their megastations to rot in the drifting cloud, and where the trail runs out into the deepest dark. Nebulae everywhere, few ports, and something always a jump behind you.

landmarks.landmark: A derelict from the age of giants - a Torgoth station built to a scale no modern hull needs, running dark for a thousand years, holding the Beacon's Voice in its dead heart. Scavengers picked the trail up first - a light bounty-hunter wing loiters in the wreck's shadow.
A Kralien megastructure from their own age of giants, adrift and cold, holding the Beacon's Lens. A Torgoth reclamation party gutted the outer decks and lingers - they want it too.
A lone relay drowned in bruise-violet nebula, older than the ruins that pointed here, holding the Beacon's Cipher. Whatever else hunts the trail got here first: a hard escort loiters in the murk.

Not a ruin - a kill-yard. XORN dragged the Beacon's Heart here and left a heavy screen on it, wrecks and mines and a wing that answers to no flag but its own. Scan the Heart and run; this is the last place to be caught still.

The deepest dark past every chart - a vast silent array adrift where the giants first lit the Beacon a thousand years ago, wrapped in a bruise-red shroud. It has waited this long. XORN will arrive fast to see it never sings again.

A ramshackle bazaar bolted together from a dozen dead hulls and strung with salvage lights - Crazy Eddy's Spaceship Emporium, where the frontier comes to buy what nobody else will sell. Hail the station to browse his deals in person; the Resonance Cradle and the good stuff live here. (He's on the Ultra-Beam too, but drop by - Eddy likes an audience.)
